item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is fantastic to me, an anomalous gum that gives you uncontrollable Gum-Gum fruit-like abilities. 😁

That's all I needed, until the fridge horror of what could have possibly stopped that thing moving at that immeasurable speed! And what if whatever it is decides that or "unprovoked attack" need a little response…?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great SCP, and you nailed the Welsh diction. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thoroughly enjoyed this. A novel concept elevated by logs, addenda, and an effective ending.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now here is something I would like to discuss:

The arm gets faster, as long as it is not stopped.

It got fast enough to not only go through but possibly even CAUSE a supernova.

At that point, it was presumably in the double digits above lightspeed. For years.

Considering all of this, and the fact that the arms speed increased EXPONENTIALLY, which is a very fun and dangerous word in physics, let alone paraphysics (was that the term?)

What did the arm hit, that stopped it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Towards the end of the description, it mentions that the affected subject gets "reeled in" once the arm reaches something it can't punch through. Once I saw that his arm was extending into the sky, the fact that he'd eventually get pulled into space was not an unexpected twist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept here is acceptable, and is introduced well. There are some areas where this could be cleaned up. For instance, the subject is designated -1, but we refer to only a specific instance (-2) in containment.

The interviews are fine, not my favorite dialogue. The issue for me was the ending. It kind of came out of nowhere, and just because the arms could move at superluminal speeds doesn't mean they themselves could as well. Also, you wouldn't really have a sonic boom moving at those speeds, you'd instead be dealing with [Cherenkov radiation https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cherenkov_radiation] (though you do hint at anomalous temporal effects).

But overall that detail at the end took me out of it. The overall execution was weak enough that overall, this is -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great stuff, the interviews really did it for me. +1

item.title: Re: My first SCP
item.content:encoded:
This concept is fantastic! Absolutely loved it, keep it up!

What I really like about this, is the humour within the story. My friend you've gotta +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah it sat in the draft forums for a while longer than I'd have liked haha.

Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… faintly remember reading this. I think I originally saw this on the forums? I don't know.

Anyways, nice work! I liked this skip!

item.title: Re: My first SCP
item.content:encoded:
Hey thanks so much for the kind words, and taking the time to suggest those edits, it means a lot!

I just finished making the changes you suggested, I agree that they make it work better.

I'm excited to write more stuff

item.title: Re: My first SCP
item.content:encoded:
You have a nice little idea here, not too long, and just the right amount of exposition to drive it home, so +1 from me, there were a few things I noticed in the article that could use an edit.

Starting with the recovery log:

The amputation wound fully healed within seconds.

From the remainder of the article, I understand this is in part due to the nature of 5021, however, I think this could be phrased better, like "The amputation of the subjects arm was observed to heal at a rapidly accelerated rate during recovery", or something like that, wording is key after all.

Next, in the first collapsible addendum:

Subject was unwilling to communicate for weeks following this session

Specification of the amount of time I think would be better, maybe something like 3 or 5 weeks as a number to put there? Just using the word "weeks" isnt very specific.

Aside from that, it's a good article, good job, and Happy Writing on your next idea!

item.title: My first SCP
item.content:encoded:
So this is my very first SCP, and after fussing over it for months I think it's time.

Thanks to Doctor Fullham for critiquing my concept and thanks to Uncle Nicolini for their draft critique.

And a big thank you to cybersqyd and RockTeethMothEyes for their in depth critique.

I'm nervous for how the reaction will be, but regardless of the outcome, the community has been very cool and I hope to stick around a while.
